headline: Warren Buffett Praises Greg Abel's Leadership at Berkshire, Says 'He's Doing Everything I Did, And Doing It Better'
summary: On Saturday Warren Buffett used Berkshire Hathaway Inc. (NYSE: BRK-A) (NYSE: BRK-B) annual meeting stage to highlight the leadership handoff that executives described as a clean win, with the board's succession choice approved without dissent and Greg Abel already running the playbook.

headline: Grandma's $1000 College Fund Gift Turned Him Into A Bitcoin Millionaire By Age 18: Here's How This High School Dropout Made It Happen
summary: Venture capitalist and entrepreneur Erik Finman made a bet with his parents that if he became a millionaire by 18, they wouldn’t force him to college.

headline: Top Economist Reportedly Warns US Recession Is Imminent, Says Major Correction Coming In Near Future
summary: Gary Shilling warned of a potential 30% drop in the S&P 500 due to ongoing economic vulnerabilities and rising oil prices.

headline: CEO Pay Surges 11% While Workers' Wages Stagnate at 0.5% in 2025: Report
summary: According to a new study, global chief executives widened the gap with workers in 2025, with CEO compensation rising far faster than wage growth and leaving inflation-adjusted worker pay lower than it was in 2019.

headline: Buffett: Apple's Leadership Shift Could Reshape Shareholder Returns Strategies, Says Cook 'Succeeded A Legend'
summary: Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL) was highlighted on Saturday after Warren Buffett described how Berkshire Hathaway's roughly $35 billion bet on the iPhone maker a decade ago ballooned to about $185 billion on a pre-tax basis, counting dividends and gains, largely under CEO Tim Cook.

headline: Elon Musk Once Revealed He Picks Deadlines He's Only Half Likely To Make And Why He's 'Totally Fine With That'
summary: Elon Musk sets aggressive deadlines with 50% chance of being met to push for speed and innovation at Tesla and SpaceX.

headline: Spirit Collapse Sparks Scramble As Rivals Rush In With Rescue Fares
summary: Spirit Airlines shutdown triggers a scramble among rivals to roll out rescue fares and capture displaced demand across the U.S. travel market.

headline: Lockheed Martin Wins Space Force Interceptor Deal, Eyes 2028 Milestone
summary: Lockheed Martin shares edge higher after securing a U.S. Space Force contract for its Space-Based Interceptor program, though weak momentum and a bearish technical setup keep near-term outlook mixed.

headline: Ackman: We Favored 'Retail Allocations Over Institutional Orders'
summary: Billionaire hedge fund manager Bill Ackman has that he was clarifying why his newly listed closed-end funds, Pershing Square USA (PSUS) and Pershing Square Inc. (PS), slid after listing, pointing to how late-day trading mechanics collided with an unusually retail-heavy allocation.

headline: Warren Buffett Once Explained Qualities He Saw In His Successor—Why Finding 'The Sound' Matters More Than Genius
summary: Buffett said Greg Abel's strength as Berkshire Hathaway's successor is preserving its disciplined, long-term culture over flashy leadership.

headline: Berkshire Hathaway's Cash Pile Hits Record High as Net Earnings Surge to $10.1 Billion
summary: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. (NYSE: BRK.A) (NYSE: BRK.B) said Saturday that first-quarter 2026 net earnings attributable to shareholders climbed to $10.106 billion, more than double the $4.603 billion posted a year earlier, as operating profit rose despite investment-market swings.

headline: EXCLUSIVE: 'True Production-Scale Deployment Is Still Limited,' Says AEye CEO—A Reality Check For Robotics & EV ETFs
summary: AEye CEO says lidar is "moving beyond validation" into early deployments, but "production-scale deployment is still limited," raising questions for ETFs betting on autonomous tech growth.

headline: Voice AI Is Heating Up—SoundHound Is Back On Investors' Radar
summary: SoundHound AI Inc is advancing its conversational AI strategy and strengthening capabilities ahead of earnings, even as mixed technical signals and sector divergence keep the near-term outlook uncertain.

headline: Spirit Airlines Shuts Down After Creditors Reject Rescue Plan
summary: Spirit Airlines (OTC: FLYYQ) stopped flying on Saturday after its creditors wouldn't back a U.S. government rescue plan, leaving the carrier to liquidate as the Iran war sent jet fuel costs sharply higher.

headline: Filmmaker Pavel Talankin's Oscar Goes Missing After TSA Forces Check-In At JFK— Then Found In Germany
summary: Pavel Talankin's Oscar went missing after TSA forced him to check it at JFK, but Lufthansa later recovered the statuette in Frankfurt.

headline: US Naval Blockade Has Cost Iran $4.8 Billion In Oil Revenue: Report
summary: The U.S. naval blockade has cost Iran around $4.8 billion in oil revenue since April 13, according to Pentagon estimates.

summary: Trump rejected Iran's latest proposal, prolonging nuclear tensions and global economic risks tied to the Strait of Hormuz.
